# Pulse HR

> Open-source workspace for the IC. Your best work is buried in a Slack thread from March; Pulse pulls it out. Three lines a day in Status Log, a kudos that sticks, a growth trail you carry into your next conversation. Source-available under FSL-1.1-MIT (converts to MIT in two years). Self-host in 90 seconds with `docker compose up`, or pay us to host. The demo is the sales call.

## Overview
- [Product tour](https://pulsehr.it/product) — Eight surfaces built around the IC: Status Log, Growth, Kudos, Moments, Workload check-in, Leave journal, Pulse, People Insights.
- [What's inside](https://pulsehr.it/modules) — One section per surface, plain-English purpose, what we deliberately don't ship.
- [Labs](https://pulsehr.it/labs) — Status Log, Kudos, Workload check-in, Moments.
- [What we improve on](https://pulsehr.it/vs) — Pulse versus the pattern of invisibility: the shared doc nobody opens, the kudos that scrolls off, the review that fits a year into a textarea. No named competitors.
- [Changelog](https://pulsehr.it/changelog) — Reverse-chronological product updates.
- [Security](https://pulsehr.it/security) — GDPR-compliant by design, EU data residency, SOC 2 Type II in progress. Sentiment data never crosses the manager line.
- [Source code](https://github.com/davide97g/pulse-hr) — Public repository, FSL-1.1-MIT license.

## Principles
- **Employee-first** — The IC is the user; the manager is a witness. Every feature is judged on whether it makes the IC's first ten minutes better, their proof of work stronger, their next conversation easier.
- **Open source** — Full source on GitHub under FSL-1.1-MIT, converts to MIT after 2 years. Read it, run it, fork it. The OSS is the whole product. No enterprise tier with extra features. No demo gate.
- **Manager-safe sentiment** — Raw chat and individual workload check-ins stay with the employee. Managers see aggregated trends only, and the code that enforces the boundary is in this repo.
- **Async-first** — Status Log is three lines in writing; Workload is one emoji tap; Pulse is anonymous and weekly. No meetings required to use the product.
- **Keyboard-first** — ⌘K fuzzy search, ⌘J command bar, 40+ shortcuts. Every workflow reachable without a mouse.
- **Built by the people who use it** — The maintainers (Davide & Niccolò) run Pulse on themselves daily. Roadmap shaped by pull requests, not product managers.

## Command bar (⌘J)
⌘J opens a local intent parser. Type "send kudos to Marta for the demo" or "log a day off last Friday" and the parser maps it to a runnable action. No LLM call, no cross-tenant training, no network dependency — works fully offline on the PWA.

## Offline (PWA)
Installs as a PWA on macOS, Windows, iOS, Android. Recent views, status log drafts and kudos drafts work offline and sync on reconnect. Destructive actions require a live connection and queue if offline.

## What Pulse does not ship
No time tracking. No timesheet approval. No project codes. No client billing. No recruiting kanban. No onboarding workflow. No e-signature. No payroll. No expense reports. No turnover %. No enterprise tier with extra features. The shorter list is the product.
